trips.name: Algarve Golden Coast
trips.description: Portugal's sun-drenched southern coast delivers the country's most spectacular scenery: towering ochre sea stacks, hidden grottos, and wide golden beaches that vanish into the Atlantic at Sagres. Drive west from the Roman-influenced town of Faro through Lagos and the bizarre rock formations of Ponta da Piedade to Europe's most south-westerly point.
trips.cover_search_query: Ponta da Piedade Lagos Portugal golden cliffs sea stacks

trips.name: Douro Valley Wine Route
trips.description: The Douro Valley is a UNESCO World Heritage landscape of terraced vineyards carved from schist slopes above one of Europe's most beautiful rivers. Drive the dramatic N222 — named the world's best road by Automobile Magazine — stopping at quintas for port wine tastings and watching the evening light turn the terraces to gold.

stops.name: Vila Nova de Foz Côa, Portugal
stops.label: Foz Côa

highlights: Foz Côa rock art UNESCO site
trips.cover_search_query: Douro Valley Portugal terraced vineyards river wine quintas

trips.name: Porto & the Minho Green Wine Country
trips.description: North of Porto the verdant Minho region produces the refreshing vinho verde — young, slightly sparkling white wine — from vines trained up granite trellises above flower-filled meadows. Drive through Lima and Minho river valleys, visit the pilgrim city of Braga, and climb to the hilltop sanctuary of Bom Jesus for sweeping Atlantic views.
trips.cover_search_query: Porto Portugal ribeira waterfront colourful buildings Douro

trips.name: Sintra, Cascais & the Setúbal Peninsula
trips.description: A fairy-tale loop from Lisbon through some of Europe's most romanticised coast. Sintra's colourful palaces and Moorish castle sit in forested hills above the Atlantic; Cascais is a chic fishing-town-turned-resort with wild beaches nearby; the Arrábida Natural Park is a hidden gem of turquoise coves and white limestone cliffs.
trips.cover_search_query: Sintra Portugal Pena Palace colourful fairy tale forest

trips.name: Alentejo Plains & Ancient Megaliths
trips.description: The Alentejo occupies one third of Portugal but barely a tenth of its people — a landscape of immense cork oak forests, golden wheat plains, and whitewashed hilltop villages baking under a merciless sun. This region hides Europe's greatest concentration of megalithic monuments, with stone circles and dolmens scattered across the plain near Évora.
trips.cover_search_query: Évora Portugal Roman temple cork oak plains Alentejo

trips.name: Costa Vicentina — Portugal's Wild Atlantic Coast
trips.description: The Costa Vicentina is Western Europe's largest stretch of undeveloped Atlantic coast — a protected park of wind-sculpted cliffs, empty surf beaches, and fishing villages barely touched by tourism. Drive the EN268 through rolling heath and dune valleys between Sines and Sagres, stopping at dramatic viewpoints above pounding winter swells.
trips.cover_search_query: Costa Vicentina Portugal wild Atlantic cliffs surf beach

trips.name: Serra da Estrela & the Beiras
trips.description: The Serra da Estrela is mainland Portugal's highest mountain range — a granite plateau of glacial valleys, ancient shepherding traditions, and the country's only ski station. Drive past schist villages clinging to steep hillsides in the Aldeias de Xisto, taste the world-famous Serra cheese, and visit the medieval walled city of Monsanto perched on its granite boulders.
trips.cover_search_query: Serra da Estrela Portugal mountain glacial valley granite

trips.name: Trás-os-Montes — The Forgotten Northeast
trips.description: Beyond the mountains, Trás-os-Montes ('behind the mountains') is Portugal's most isolated region — a land of chestnut forests, ancient villages, and the dramatic Tua and Sabor river gorges. Chaves has Roman bridges and thermal springs; Bragança guards the Spanish border behind its perfectly intact medieval castle and walls.
trips.cover_search_query: Bragança Portugal medieval castle citadel fortress

trips.name: Silver Coast & Coimbra University Town
trips.description: The Costa de Prata stretches between Lisbon and Porto — a silver-sand coast of fishing towns, pine forests, and Atlantic surf largely ignored by international tourism. Inland, Coimbra's ancient university city rises over the Mondego river with the finest Manueline architecture in Portugal and a fado tradition wholly different from Lisbon's.
trips.cover_search_query: Coimbra Portugal university Joanina library Mondego river

trips.name: Peneda-Gerês National Park
trips.description: Portugal's only national park is a rugged granite wilderness of waterfalls, oak forests, and wild Garrano ponies roaming free above Celtic-era villages. The Cávado and Lima river valleys cut through the park, dotted with ancient stone bridges, Roman milestones, and terraced fields worked by communities largely unchanged for centuries.
trips.cover_search_query: Peneda Gerês Portugal national park waterfall granite wild ponies

trips.name: Guimarães & Braga — Cradle of Portugal
trips.description: Guimarães is where Portugal was born — the first capital of the kingdom, its medieval centre so perfectly preserved it is a UNESCO World Heritage site. Nearby Braga is the country's religious capital, home to dozens of baroque churches and the extraordinary pilgrimage staircase of Bom Jesus, flanked by symbolic chapels representing the Stations of the Cross.
trips.cover_search_query: Guimarães Portugal medieval castle birthplace nation UNESCO

trips.name: Alentejo Wine & Marble Towns
trips.description: The Alentejo produces Portugal's most celebrated red wines — big, structured, and deeply aromatic from the blazing summer heat. The marble triangle of Estremoz, Borba, and Vila Viçosa is a unique landscape where entire town centres are built from dazzling white stone quarried nearby, and ducal palaces rival anything in Lisbon.
trips.cover_search_query: Alentejo Portugal wine estate marble whitewashed village

trips.name: Óbidos, Templars & Tomar
trips.description: A compact circuit through central Portugal's most romantic medieval towns. Óbidos is an impossibly picturesque walled village gifted by Portuguese kings to their queens; Alcobaça hosts Portugal's finest gothic abbey; and Tomar's Convent of Christ — headquarters of the Knights Templar in Portugal — is one of the country's supreme UNESCO monuments.
trips.cover_search_query: Tomar Convent of Christ Portugal Knights Templar UNESCO

trips.name: Lisbon & the Tagus Estuary
trips.description: Lisbon rewards slow exploration — fado echoing down steep alleys, Manueline stonework glowing at Belém, and sweeping views from Alfama's hilltop miradouros. Cross the 25 de Abril bridge to the Setúbal Peninsula, where the UNESCO-listed rice paddies and flamingo lagoons of the Sado estuary offer an unexpected wilderness close to the capital.
trips.cover_search_query: Lisbon Portugal Belém tower Tagus river sunset

trips.name: Beira Baixa & Castle Country
trips.description: The border country between Portugal and Spain is defined by granite ridges and ancient frontier castles guarding every hilltop along the Tagus river. Castelo Branco has one of Portugal's most extraordinary baroque gardens; the twin villages of Marvão and Castelo de Vide perch dramatically on rocky spurs above the plain.
trips.cover_search_query: Marvão Portugal clifftop village medieval castle fortress

trips.name: Setúbal Peninsula & Moscatel Wine
trips.description: Just south of Lisbon, the triangular Setúbal Peninsula hides one of Portugal's great wine surprises — the muscatel vineyards of Azeitão, producing rich amber dessert wines from ancient Moscatel vines. Combine cellar visits with a walk through Arrábida's limestone gorge to an impossibly clear turquoise cove that looks more like the Aegean than the Atlantic.
trips.cover_search_query: Arrábida Portugal turquoise cove limestone cliffs Setúbal

trips.name: Ribatejo — Bullfighting & Tagus Plains
trips.description: The Ribatejo is Portugal's flat heartland — a vast Tagus floodplain where white Lusitano horses, black fighting bulls, and campino horsemen in traditional red waistcoats define the landscape. Santarém is the 'Capital of Gothic' with an extraordinary density of medieval churches; Abrantes guards the Tagus from a hilltop castle with sweeping river views.
trips.cover_search_query: Santarém Portugal Gothic tower Tagus river plains

trips.name: Eastern Algarve & Ria Formosa Lagoon
trips.description: The eastern Algarve is calmer and wilder than its western counterpart — the Ria Formosa Natural Park is a vast lagoon of islands, salt pans, and mudflats stretching 60km along the coast, home to flamingos, purple gallinules, and the rare purple heron. Take a boat to the barrier islands, explore Tavira's beautiful old town, and cross to Spain at Vila Real.

stops.name: Vila Real de Santo António, Portugal
stops.label: Vila Real

trips.cover_search_query: Ria Formosa Portugal lagoon barrier islands flamingos Algarve

trips.name: Serra de Monchique — Algarve Highlands
trips.description: Rising to 902m above the Algarve coast, the Serra de Monchique is a lush anomaly in an otherwise arid region — rain-fed forests of eucalyptus and medronho arbutus trees, cool spa towns, and dramatic views from Foia's summit over both the Atlantic and the Alentejo plains. A complete contrast to the beach crowds a mere 30 minutes away.
trips.cover_search_query: Serra de Monchique Portugal highlands forest Foia summit Algarve

trips.name: Ave & Cávado — Industrial Valleys to Romanesque Routes
trips.description: The river valleys of Ave and Cávado powered Portugal's textile industry and produced its greatest Romanesque monuments. The route passes through Guimarães' medieval birthplace, the extraordinary Romanesque cathedral of Braga, and the prehistoric stone citânia of Briteiros — an entire Celtic town preserved on a hilltop above the Minho valley.
highlights: Textile heritage of the Ave valley
trips.cover_search_query: Citânia de Briteiros Portugal Celtic hillfort ruins Minho
